text.title: AGENTS.md Per-Module Bootstrap Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding AGENTS.md Per-Module Bootstrap. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Every source-containing dir has CLAUDE.md (=@AGENTS.md) + AGENTS.md.
rule.rationale: Every source-containing dir has CLAUDE.md (=@AGENTS.md) + AGENTS.md.

rule.statement: AGENTS.md is 20–80 lines; longer goes to .agents/.
rule.rationale: AGENTS.md is 20–80 lines; longer goes to .agents/.

rule.statement: AGENTS.md has: identity paragraph, file table, key types, gotchas.
rule.rationale: AGENTS.md has: identity paragraph, file table, key types, gotchas.

rule.statement: Empty __init__.py-only dirs are the only allowed skip.
rule.rationale: Empty __init__.py-only dirs are the only allowed skip.

rule.statement: CLAUDE.md contents must be exactly `@AGENTS.md` — no other content.
rule.rationale: CLAUDE.md contents must be exactly `@AGENTS.md` — no other content.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-agents-md-per-module-bootstrap.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
